pattern: Marking each guard's line of sight by sweeping outward in all four directions until a wall or guard blocks it lets a final grid scan tally the cells that stay both empty and unseen.
hints: Build the grid, place walls and guards, then have each guard illuminate cells until something stops the beam.
A guard's sight in one direction halts the moment it hits a wall or another guard, so process each direction independently.
After casting all sightlines, count cells that are neither a guard, a wall, nor marked as seen.

edge_inputs.description: guard fully boxed in by walls

stress.description: random mid-size grid with disjoint guard and wall placements
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 60)))
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 60)))
    cells = [(r, c) for r in range(m) for c in range(n)]
    rng.shuffle(cells)
    total = len(cells)
    ng = rng.randint(1, max(1, total // 4))
    nw = rng.randint(1, max(1, total // 4))
    guards = [list(c) for c in cells[:ng]]
    walls = [list(c) for c in cells[ng:ng + nw]]
    return [m, n, guards, walls]
stress.note: guards and walls drawn from a shuffled cell list stay unique and disjoint
